Title: Metabolic engineering-induced transcriptome reprogramming of lipid biosynthesis enhances oil composition in oat

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most endogenous AsDGAT genes showed higher expression in transgenic lines than WT, especially in D35A leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated up to ~1-fold in D35A leaves; minimal increases in D23 seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous DGAT homologs were transcriptionally induced by the transgene stack, consistent with enhanced oil biosynthetic capacity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seven endogenous AsDGAT genes were identified in the oat genome. 2. Most showed higher transcript abundance in transgenic lines than WT. 3. Their induction supports transgene-triggered reinforcement of TAG assembly pathways.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed versus boot leaf tissue comparison
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous response genes, not primary transformation targets.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates fatty acid biosynthesis relevant to oil quality
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: One homolog AVESA.00001b.r3.5Cg0001408 increased most strongly, about 2.5-fold in D23 seeds and 4.6-fold in D35A leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong induction in transgenic tissues relative to WT
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced endogenous WRI1 expression suggests amplification of lipid regulatory circuitry in transgenic oat
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Three AsWRI1 genes were identified. 2. AVESA.00001b.r3.5Cg0001408 was the most strongly upregulated endogenous lipid regulator in transgenic tissues. 3. This supports transcriptional reinforcement of fatty acid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed and boot leaf tissue context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous homolog response rather than direct genetic manipulation.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increased expression detected in D23 seeds but not D35A leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous OLEOSIN upregulation in seeds is consistent with enhanced oil body formation/storage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Four AsOLEOSIN genes were identified. 2. Their induction was observed in D23 seeds but not D35A leaves. 3. This seed-specific response aligns with storage lipid packaging in engineered oat grains.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mature seed versus boot leaf tissue comparison
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Responsive endogenous storage genes rather than primary engineered targets.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Pisum sativum vicilin promoter (seed-preferred) in pJP3679; CaMV-35S constitutive promoter in pJP3502
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms storage oil (TAG) that determines oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved nutritional oil quality and potential shelf-life stability through higher oleic acid proportion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in both seeds and leaves; highest in D35A seeds among transgenes and detectable ectopically beyond intended tissue specificity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to increased oleic acid proportion in oat grains and increased leaf oil content when co-expressed with AtWRI1 and SiOLEOSIN
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal TAG assembly enzyme; used as one of three key lipid engineering genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable oat transformation; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling by GC and oil quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDGAT1 was stably expressed in transgenic oat seeds and leaves. 2. Its expression as part of the three-gene stack was associated with transcriptome reprogramming and upregulation of endogenous DGAT homologs. 3. Co-expression increased grain oleic acid content up to 34.33% and increased leaf oil content in several transgenic lines.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced protein content in seeds and leaves; no major morphological penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal oil-assembly gene for stacking with WRI1 and OLEOSIN to improve oat oil composition and vegetative oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; tissue context (mature seed versus boot leaf) strongly affected response
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotypic effects were evaluated in combination with AtWRI1 and SiOLEOSIN, so single-gene contribution was not isolated.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Phaseolus vulgaris phaseolin storage protein promoter in pJP3679; Arabidopsis thaliana Rubisco small subunit promoter in pJP3502
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Lipid droplet / oil body assembly and storage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/sequestration of target oil rather than catalysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports storage of nutritionally improved oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in both seeds and leaves; highest among transgenes in D35A leaves and high in D22/D23 seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported increased oil accumulation in leaves and improved seed oil composition when co-expressed with AtDGAT1 and AtWRI1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable oat transformation; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; oil and fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiOLEOSIN was robustly expressed in transgenic oat tissues, especially D35A leaves. 2. Its expression was associated with increased endogenous OLEOSIN transcripts in seeds. 3. In the three-gene stack, it contributed to improved oil accumulation/storage without visible morphological defects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced protein content accompanied oil engineering; no major growth penalty observed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable storage-factor gene for engineering high-oil oat tissues together with DGAT1 and WRI1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue-specific context influenced transgene and endogenous response
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not an enzyme, it is one of the three experimentally central transgenes driving the phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Phaseolus vulgaris phaseolin storage protein promoter in pJP3679; Arabidopsis thaliana Rubisco small subunit promoter in pJP3502
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of lipid biosynthetic programs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid biosynthesis and oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous AsWRI1-associated lipid pathway genes; AsDGAT; AsOLEOSIN; KASII; SACPD; FAD2 and broader lipid metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overall induced transcriptional reprogramming; endogenous AsWRI1 strongly upregulated; fatty acid biosynthetic genes generally up in seeds and down in leaves depending on tissue context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key lipid regulatory gene used to drive broad transcriptional reprogramming toward altered oil composition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable oat transformation; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq differential expression; GC fatty acid profiling; oil quantification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to increased grain oleic acid content and altered global lipid-gene expression when co-expressed with AtDGAT1 and SiOLEOSIN
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves oil nutritional quality and potentially storage stability
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in both seed and leaf tissues; higher in D35A than pJP3679 lines, indicating ectopic expression in oat
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced protein content in engineered tissues; no major morphological changes observed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulatory hub for stacked engineering of healthier oat oil profiles
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context strongly influenced downstream responses in seed versus leaf
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRI1 expression in oat caused broad transcriptome reprogramming enriched for lipid metabolism. 2. Endogenous AsWRI1 homolog AVESA.00001b.r3.5Cg0001408 was strongly induced in transgenic seed and leaf tissues. 3. In the three-gene stack, this regulator was associated with increased oleic acid and altered primary metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study tested AtWRI1 only in combination with AtDGAT1 and SiOLEOSIN, so stand-alone regulatory contribution was not dissected.
